method: multi-faith: hypothesize capacity rules, test which derives the GR profile (dp 2026-06-22)
verdict: MULTI-FAITH ON CAPACITY RULES — which derives GR? FAITH A (momentum-coupled, rho ∝ v^n): sweeping the coupling n, the density exponent is -2n/(n+1) and the light deflection scales b^-q; ONLY n=3 lands on GR — rho ∝ r^-3/2 AND Delta_theta ∝ 1/b (GR faith: n=3, density_exp=-1.5, q=1.016). The others are abandoned-for-GR but real: n=0 (incompressible) -> b^-4 (Phase-7's wrong case); n=1,2 -> too-steep deflection; n>3 -> too shallow. So the capacity rule GR REQUIRES is sharp and falsifiable: 'a cell's held intent scales as the CUBE of its flow speed' (rho ∝ v^3) — derived from continuity, NO gravitational force postulated. That is real narrowing: gravity-is-fit has shrunk from 'fit the inflow' (3c) to 'fit the EoS' (Phase-7) to 'the momentum-coupling exponent is exactly 3' (here). What we have NOT done: derive WHY n=3 from the cell's actual receive-dynamics — that is the open question, now a single number. FAITH B (saturating capacity, rho <= I_max): clipping only cores the CENTRE (r < r_sat=0.342); the tail stays rho ∝ r^-1.5 (still GR: True). REDIRECT, not abandon: a cored-centre + GR-tail profile is exactly what galactic dark-matter HALOS look like (the core-cusp structure) — so the saturating capacity rule is worth keeping for the galactic (P4/B2) frontier, where it would change rotation curves WITHOUT changing solar-system lensing. HONEST: power-law EoS -> power-law profile is textbook fluid analog; finding n=3 is LOCATING the rule, not deriving it (numerological until the receive-dynamics force n=3). Bucket 0 unchanged. But the multi-faith did its job: one path (n=3) is the GR target, one (saturation) redirects to the galactic frontier, the rest are eliminated.
